summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of the static Johnson City Observer Community Stewardship Hub (main page + /data-center.html). All 19 test scenarios in the review request PASSED including masthead/dateline rendering, Heritage & Culture articles, Meeting Tracker filter buttons (Zoning/Utilities/Budgets/All), live text search (annexation), navigation links, all 11 Data Center modules, interactive sliders (electric/water/sound/air/stranded), water bar resizing, SVG home marker horizontal translation, four file downloads (RTK .txt, Ordinance .txt, Comment .txt, Survey .csv), comment drafter with name/hood interpolation, acoustic survey dB display, and all four scripture <details> accordions expanding.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical issues. Static HTML/CSS/JS implementation is clean, all data-testid attributes properly placed, computations responsive, downloads use proper Blob anchors. Minor observation: React App.js returns null as intended so CRA dev server does not interfere with /public/index.html — this is by design per the requirements.
seed_data_creation: N/A — 15 council findings are seeded in index.html markup
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 11 modules verified working. Module 1 (electric slider 250->500 MW): loadPct 27.8%->55.6%, infra $312.5M->$625M, monthly $13.71->$27.41. Module 2 (water): readouts update AND bar widths resize. Module 3 (sound dist 200->500->50 ft): #house SVG transform translate(146,140)->translate(200,140)->translate(119,140) confirms horizontal translation. Module 7 (air gens slider): fuel/NOx/PM all update. Module 9 (stranded): half-life, strand %, blight risk class label updates. Downloads: right-to-know-checklist.txt, model-hyperscale-data-center-ordinance.txt, public-comment-draft.txt (contains 'Jane Q. Resident' & 'Cherry Street'), acoustic-survey-<timestamp>.csv all trigger correctly via page.expect_download. All four scripture <details> open on click. Filter buttons correctly toggle visibility by data-category attribute. Navigation links nav-data-center, banner-arrow, nav-home, footer-home-link all route correctly between / and /data-center.html.
